commands.review_summary:
Bounds Matrix raw transport reads with a default SDK cap, forwards encrypted-media download options through the crypto facade, reorders encrypted media download before native crypto binding load, and adds Matrix SDK regression tests.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +136. Total +140 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main routes raw Matrix requests with omitted maxBytes to response.arrayBuffer(), and encrypted-media limits are dropped at the crypto facade callback. I did not run a live Matrix homeserver repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review remains because the missing action is contributor runtime proof plus maintainer acceptance or tuning of the compatibility boundary, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(matrix): bound raw transport response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Matrix raw transport responses with omitted `maxBytes` called `response.arrayBuffer()`, buffering the entire response body without any size guard. This is an OOM vector against untrusted Matrix homeservers. Additionally, encrypted media download size limits (`maxBytes`/`readIdleTimeoutMs`) were silently dropped at the crypto facade → download callback boundary, and the expensive native crypto binding load ran before the download could fail on oversized media.

## Why This Change Was Made

Three related fixes in the Matrix transport/crypto chain:

1. **`transport.ts`**: Always bounds raw response reads with a default cap (reuses the existing `MATRIX_SDK_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES` = 64 MiB, already applied in `createMatrixGuardedFetch`). The previously-unbounded `response.arrayBuffer()` fallback is removed entirely. Callers with legitimate extra-large media must pass an explicit `maxBytes`.

2. **`sdk.ts`**: Forwards `opts` through the crypto facade download callback into `this.downloadContent(mxcUrl, opts)` instead of dropping them at `this.downloadContent(mxcUrl)`. This was the last link in the chain: `monitor/media.ts` already passes `maxBytes`/`readIdleTimeoutMs` to `decryptMedia`, but those values never reached `performMatrixRequest`.

3. **`crypto-facade.ts`**: Reorders `deps.downloadContent(file.url, opts)` before `loadMatrixCryptoNodeBindings()`, so oversized encrypted media fails at the transport size guard without paying the expensive native binding load first.

## User Impact

- Matrix raw downloads above 64 MiB with omitted `maxBytes` now fail closed instead of buffering unbounded data (potential OOM).
- Encrypted media size limits now actually reach the transport layer — previously they were silently ineffective.
- Explicit caller `maxBytes` values continue to work as before.

## Evidence

Full Matrix extension test suite (all 1420 tests pass, 0 failures):

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/ -- --run
Test Files  123 passed (123)
Tests  1420 passed (1420)
```

Targeted transport proof (15 tests, 3 new):

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/transport.test.ts -- --run
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  15 passed (15)
```

Crypto facade options-forwarding proof:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk.test.ts -- --run -t "forwards encrypted media"
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 84 skipped (85)
```

## Related

- PR #97662 — independent contributor submission covering similar ground; this PR applies the same fixes in a focused maintainer-authored change with full suite proof.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
